title: Synapse — browser deep-learning lab
description: A deep-learning framework built from scratch on a real reverse-mode tensor autograd engine (no TensorFlow.js) — 30+ hand-derived ops including conv2d, max/avg pooling, LayerNorm, BatchNorm, dropout, GELU/SiLU, embedding, a full softmax Jacobian, and a fused BCE-with-logits + closed-form Gaussian KL, every one proven correct by a one-click engine self-test that gradchecks it (and a whole Transformer, a whole VAE AND a whole diffusion denoiser end-to-end) to ~1e-7. Ten labs share the engine: a 2-D Playground (sketch an MLP, train it live on spirals/checkerboards with AdamW/Nesterov, schedules and clipping, and watch decision boundaries, neuron feature maps, train-vs-val curves and a live computation graph); a Vision lab that trains a from-scratch CNN on a fully procedural image dataset (handwritten-style digits 0–9 and shapes) with live filter/feature-map views, a confusion matrix and a draw-your-own pad; a Transformer · Attention lab that trains a from-scratch decoder-only GPT on procedural algorithmic tasks (copy/reverse/sort/add) and shows the live per-layer, per-head causal attention maps, greedy decoding coloured against ground truth, a type-your-own decoder with confidence bars, and a token-embedding PCA; a Generative · VAE lab that trains a from-scratch variational autoencoder (reparameterization trick + KL) on those glyphs and renders a live navigable 2-D latent manifold of synthesised digits, side-by-side reconstructions, a class-coloured latent-space scatter, samples drawn from the prior, latent interpolation between two glyphs, and a slider-driven latent explorer; a Diffusion · DDPM/DDIM lab that trains a from-scratch denoising diffusion model on those glyphs — a time-conditioned residual-MLP that predicts the noise (the Ho et al. ε-MSE objective) over a linear or cosine schedule, with classifier-free label dropout — and lets you watch the headline reverse trajectory (a glyph condensing out of pure Gaussian noise step by step, with the model's live x̂₀ prediction beside it and a scrubber), sample with DDIM (fast, deterministic, η-tunable) or ancestral DDPM, steer with classifier-free guidance, browse a class-conditional sample sheet, spherically interpolate between two noise seeds through the deterministic DDIM map, and read the noise schedule (ᾱ_t / β_t / log-SNR) off a plot — with the denoiser gradchecked end-to-end and the schedule, posterior and DDIM identities proven to machine precision; and a Control · RL lab that trains a from-scratch policy-gradient agent (REINFORCE, REINFORCE+baseline, advantage actor–critic with GAE, and PPO with the clipped surrogate, multi-epoch minibatch SGD and clip-fraction/approx-KL/explained-variance diagnostics) on four hand-written environments — CartPole and a GridWorld maze (discrete), plus Pendulum (a continuous-torque swing-up driven by a diagonal-Gaussian policy with a learnable log-σ) and MountainCar (sparse reward made learnable with optimality-preserving potential-based shaping) — and shows the live agent acting frame-by-frame, the episode-return + policy-entropy learning curves, a per-batch return-distribution histogram, the live action distribution (a μ ± σ gauge for continuous control), and an env-specific portrait (the CartPole phase-portrait, the GridWorld value heatmap, the Pendulum torque field, or the MountainCar position×velocity map). Continuous control added rowSum, gaussianLogProb and gaussianEntropy — all hand-derived and gradchecked alongside whole-policy end-to-end checks for both the discrete and the continuous actor, for 48 self-tested ops in all. The newest lab — Flows · RealNVP — completes the generative trio (the VAE optimises a lower bound, diffusion learns a score, and a normalizing flow gives the EXACT likelihood): a from-scratch RealNVP coupling flow trained by exact maximum likelihood on 2-D densities (two-moons, pinwheel, spirals, circles, a Gaussian grid, checkerboard), where the change-of-variables log-determinant is hand-derived in closed form (the Jacobian is triangular, so log|det| is just the sum of the coupling log-scales) and proven against a finite-difference Jacobian, the flow's exact invertibility f⁻¹(f(x))≡x proven to 1e-16, and the whole stack gradchecked end-to-end through its negative-log-likelihood — visualised with the EXACT model density painted live as a heatmap as it pours into the data's shape, the bijective coordinate warp of latent space into the data manifold, the data's pushforward z=f(x) relaxing onto the base Gaussian's σ-rings, samples drawn the other way x=f⁻¹(z), and a live train/val NLL (and bits/dim) curve. The eighth lab — Graph · GNN — adds the first non-vector modality: a from-scratch graph neural network doing semi-supervised node classification, where only a handful of nodes per class are labeled and the network labels the whole graph by passing messages along edges. Three convolutions share the engine — GCN (the symmetric-normalized propagation Â=D̃^(-½)(A+I)D̃^(-½)), GraphSAGE (a mean neighbor aggregator), and multi-head GAT (graph attention, the per-edge score masked to real edges and row-softmaxed) — each a few dense matmuls against a precomputed propagation matrix, so every gradient is gradchecked end-to-end (GCN/SAGE/GAT all to ~1e-8, the self-test now 54 ops). It runs on procedural graphs (a Stochastic Block Model, the real Zachary Karate Club, and kNN geometric graphs over moons/rings/blobs/spirals) whose node features are deliberately a weak class signal in noise — so the headline is the graph itself, every node filled with its predicted class and ringed with its true one (a colour mismatch is a live mistake) healing as message passing sharpens the labels, with GAT edges glowing by attention; a 'use the graph' toggle collapses the model to a per-node MLP (SBM ~96% test accuracy with the graph vs ~54% without), beside a live PCA of the learned embeddings and train/val/test accuracy curves. The ninth lab — KAN · Splines — is a from-scratch Kolmogorov–Arnold Network (Liu et al., 2024), the architecture that moves the nonlinearity from the nodes to the edges: every connection carries a learned univariate function φ(x) = a SiLU base + a B-spline, and a node just sums. The whole layer is one fused autograd op whose hand-derived backward differentiates the output w.r.t. the base weights, every spline coefficient, AND the input x — the chain rule through the analytic B-spline derivative B′(x) that lets KAN layers stack (gradchecked, input path included, to ~1e-6; the self-test now 59 ops). The headline is the iconic KAN diagram — the network drawn as a graph of functions with each edge's spline rendered inline and animating as it trains, unimportant edges fading as the net prunes itself, click-to-inspect any φ with its knots marked — alongside a live 2-D decision boundary (classification) or the learned 1-D curve through noisy data (regression, where a tiny KAN nails sharp steps a same-size ReLU MLP smears, R²≈0.98). And it exposes the architecture's superpower: the B-spline basis is a partition of unity (proven to machine precision) and its coefficients can be re-solved onto a new knot vector by least squares, so a trained KAN can be refined live (×2 grid resolution) or re-centred onto its activation range without forgetting — curves preserved to ~1e-5. The tenth lab — Neural ODE · Continuous depth — replaces the layer stack with a single learned vector field: a from-scratch continuous-depth classifier where dz/dt = f_θ(z, t) and the prediction is head(z(1)), integrated by a hand-written Euler / midpoint / RK4 solver whose every step is built from the engine's own tape ops, so back-propagation runs straight THROUGH the solver and is gradchecked end-to-end (to ~6e-6). It implements the two ideas that made Neural ODEs famous: the ADJOINT METHOD — the O(1)-memory gradient obtained by integrating a second 'adjoint' ODE backwards in time, its vector–Jacobian products taken with the engine's own reverse mode — which the lab proves reproduces back-prop-through-the-solver on a live batch (to ~3e-7, tightening as you refine the step count); and AUGMENTED Neural ODEs (Dupont et al., 2019) — extra zero-initialised state channels that let trajectories leave the plane, because a 2-D ODE flow is a homeomorphism and cannot pull apart concentric rings without crossing trajectories. The headline is a single canvas fusing the terminal decision regions, the time-dependent learned vector field f_θ(·, t) as a quiver you scrub through time, and the live trajectory of every sample point flowing from t=0 → t=1 (depth made into time) — beside a 'lift' view that shows points rising off the a=0 plane into the augmented axis to route around each other, and accuracy/loss curves. RK4 exactness is proven against the closed-form solution of a linear ODE (z(1)=e^A z0, to ~4e-11), the self-test now covering 62 ops. Save, load and share experiments via a URL link.
